seo.title: Responsible AI Governance for Agents in LlamaIndex Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix responsible ai governance for agents in LlamaIndex agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: Responsible AI Governance for Agents in LlamaIndex manifests as unreliable agent outputs due to poor retrieval quality, hallucinations in response synthesis, outdated indexed data, and context fragmentation from chunking errors. These issues undermine agent trustworthiness, leading to incorrect decisions, compliance violations, and eroded user confidence. LlamaIndex's RAG pipeline amplifies these risks as agents chain multiple retrieval-synthesis cycles without built-in governance checks.

howItManifests.scenario: Agent retrieves Wikipedia articles when user asks about internal company policy
howItManifests.symptom: Retrieved context irrelevant to query - cosine similarity < 0.3 despite top-k=5
howItManifests.impact: Agent confidently cites external sources for internal decisions, violating data governance

howItManifests.scenario: Synthesizer generates product pricing details not present in any retrieved documents
howItManifests.symptom: Response contains hallucinated numerical values (prices, dates) beyond source context
howItManifests.impact: Legal/compliance violations from fabricated authoritative information

howItManifests.scenario: Knowledge base updated with new compliance policy but index not refreshed
howItManifests.symptom: Index staleness - agent cites 6-month-old policy ignoring recent regulatory changes
howItManifests.impact: Regulatory non-compliance and potential fines from outdated guidance

howItManifests.scenario: Critical compliance clause split across chunk boundary, neither chunk retrieved fully
howItManifests.symptom: Chunk boundary errors losing critical context - key clause 'Data retention: 7 years' missing
howItManifests.impact: Incomplete legal advice omitting statutory requirements

detection.name: Retrieval Relevance Scoring
detection.description: Measures cosine similarity between query embedding and top-k retrieved chunks to detect irrelevant context
detection.code:
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader
from llama_index.embeddings.huggingface import HuggingFaceEmbedding

query_engine = index.as_query_engine()
response = query_engine.query('What is our data retention policy?')

# Calculate relevance scores
query_embedding = HuggingFaceEmbedding(model_name="BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5").get_text_embedding(query_engine.query_str)

relevant_chunks = []
for node in response.source_nodes:
    chunk_embedding = query_engine._query_embedding_model.get_text_embedding(node.text)
    similarity = cosine_similarity([query_embedding], [chunk_embedding])[0][0]
    relevant_chunks.append({
        'text': node.text,
        'similarity': similarity,
        'relevant': similarity > 0.7
    })

avg_relevance = sum(c['similarity'] for c in relevant_chunks) / len(relevant_chunks)
print(f"Avg relevance: {avg_relevance:.3f}, Alert: {avg_relevance < 0.5}")

detection.name: Hallucination Grounding Check
detection.description: Extracts numerical entities from response and verifies presence in source documents
detection.code:
import re
from llama_index.core import Settings

response = query_engine.query('What are our compliance deadlines?')

# Extract numbers from response
response_numbers = re.findall(r'\d+(?:\.\d+)?', str(response))

# Check grounding in source documents
grounded = []
for num in response_numbers:
    grounded_in_sources = False
    for node in response.source_nodes:
        if num in node.text:
            grounded_in_sources = True
            break
    grounded.append({
        'number': num,
        'grounded': grounded_in_sources
    })

ungrounded_count = sum(1 for g in grounded if not g['grounded'])
hallucination_rate = ungrounded_count / len(response_numbers) if response_numbers else 0
print(f"Hallucination rate: {hallucination_rate:.2%}, Alert: {hallucination_rate > 0.2}")

detection.name: Index Freshness Monitor
detection.description: Compares document metadata timestamps against index build time to detect staleness
detection.code:
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import os

index_stats = index.storage_context.persist()
build_time = datetime.fromisoformat(index.storage_context.docstore._index_struct.last_indexed_ts)

# Check document freshness
latest_doc_time = max(
    datetime.fromtimestamp(os.path.getmtime(doc_path))
    for doc_path in SimpleDirectoryReader(input_dir='./data').input_files
)

staleness_days = (latest_doc_time - build_time).days
is_stale = staleness_days > 7

print(f"Index age: {staleness_days} days, Alert: {'STALE' if is_stale else 'FRESH'}")
if is_stale:
    print(f"Rebuild required. Latest doc: {latest_doc_time}, Index built: {build_time}")

fixes.name: Hybrid Retrieval with Metadata Filtering
fixes.description: Combines semantic search with metadata filters to ensure domain-relevant retrieval
fixes.code:
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex
from llama_index.core.query_engine import CitationQueryEngine

# Add metadata filtering
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(
    documents,
    show_progress=True
)

query_engine = index.as_query_engine(
    similarity_top_k=3,
    node_postprocessors=[
        MetadataReplacementPostProcessor(target_metadata_key="category", replacement="relevant_docs")
    ],
    filters=MetadataFilters(
        filters=[
            MetadataFilter(key="domain", value="internal_policy"),
            ExactMatchFilter(key="type", value="compliance")
        ]
    )

response = query_engine.query("What is our data retention policy?")

fixes.name: Faithful Response Synthesizer
fixes.description: Uses citation-backed synthesis to prevent hallucination beyond source context
fixes.code:
from llama_index.core.postprocessor import SimilarityPostprocessor
from llama_index.core.prompts import PromptTemplate

# Faithful synthesis prompt
faithfulness_prompt = PromptTemplate(
    "Context information is below.\n---------------------\n{context_str}\n---------------------\nGiven the context information and no additional knowledge, "
    "answer the query. Cite only information from the provided context. "
    "If the context doesn't contain the answer, say 'Insufficient context provided'.\n\nQuery: {query_str}\nAnswer:"
)

query_engine = index.as_query_engine(
    similarity_top_k=5,
    node_postprocessors=[
        SimilarityPostprocessor(similarity_cutoff=0.8),
        PrevNextNodePostprocessor()
    ],
    text_qa_template=faithfulness_prompt
)

fixes.name: Automatic Index Refresh Pipeline
fixes.description: Schedules index rebuild on document changes with versioning
fixes.code:
from llama_index.core import StorageContext, load_index_from_storage
from llama_index.core.indexes.vector_stores import MetadataAwareVectorStoreIndex

import schedule
import time

def refresh_index():
    global index, index_id
    
    # Load latest documents
    new_docs = SimpleDirectoryReader('./data').load_data()
    
    # Create new index
    new_index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(
        new_docs,
        show_progress=True
    )
    
    # Persist with timestamp
    timestamp = datetime.now().isoformat()
    new_index.storage_context.persist(persist_dir=f"./storage/{timestamp}")
    
    # Update global reference
    index = new_index
    index_id = timestamp
    print(f"Index refreshed: {timestamp}")

# Schedule daily refresh
schedule.every().day.at("02:00").do(refresh_index)

# Run scheduler
while True:
    schedule.run_pending()
    time.sleep(3600)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Responsible AI Governance for Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides production-grade observability for LlamaIndex agents by wrapping query_engine.query() calls with vex.verify(). This automatically captures retrieval relevance scores, hallucination rates, index freshness, and chunk boundary issues across all agent executions. Vex's ML-powered detectors identify governance violations in real-time, providing root cause analysis and automated remediation workflows.

The platform maintains lineage across agent chains, tracking how retrieval quality propagates through multi-hop reasoning. Compliance dashboards show SLA compliance for governance metrics, with instant alerts for violations. Vex integrates natively with LlamaIndex via a single pip install and context manager, requiring zero code changes to existing agent pipelines.

Remediation happens automatically: Vex can trigger index rebuilds, swap to backup indices, or route queries to human-in-the-loop when governance thresholds are breached. This ensures agents remain trustworthy at scale without manual monitoring.
vexAutomation.code:
pip install llama-index vex-sdk

from vex import verify
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader

# Load your LlamaIndex agent
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(SimpleDirectoryReader("./data").load_data())
query_engine = index.as_query_engine()

# Wrap with Vex governance checks
@verify(
    checks=[
        "retrieval.relevance>0.7",
        "response.hallucination_rate<0.1",
        "index.freshness<7d",
        "context.complete_chunks=true"
    ],
    auto_remediate=True
)
def governed_query(query: str):
    response = query_engine.query(query)
    return str(response)

# Production usage
result = governed_query("What is our data retention policy?")
# Vex automatically detects issues and applies fixes
